titlestmt.author: Compiled by G. Solomon, M.E. Warren

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-09-03 18:03:05 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: James J. Gibson papers
did.physdesc: Professional papers include extensive drafts, notes, outlines, letters, conferences, courses, lectures, seminars, and symposia, magazine articles, newspaper clippings, notes, and pamphlets, research papers include drafts, notes, and correspondence, typescripts of articles, books, essays, short papers, diagrams, original sketches, photographs, and tables.
did.abstract: Gibson's experimentation and research papers include drafts, notes, and correspondence in many areas of perceptual psychology including aviation, perspective, and physiological optics. Documentation for published material. Correspondence concerning Gibson's writing career.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of psychology, Smith College, 1929-1949; Cornell University, 1949-1979.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Professional papers include extensive drafts, notes, outlines, and occasional letters related to colloquia, conferences, courses, lectures, seminars, and symposia given at Smith College and Cornell; also, magazine articles, newspaper clippings, notes, and pamphlets for a course on propaganda planned for 1939. Gibson's experimentation and research papers include drafts, notes, and correspondence in many areas of perceptual psychology including aviation, perspective, and physiological optics. Documentation for published material includes drafts and typescripts of articles, books, essays, short papers, diagrams, original sketches, photographs, and tables; also, annotated reprints by Gibson and other prominent psychologists. Correspondence includes letters to and from the Houghton Mifflin Company and LEONARDO magazine concerning Gibson's writing career; with groups such as the National Academy of Sciences, the Symposium of Oral Sensation, and the Research Career Award; and with Rudolf Arnheim, Jacob Beck (University of Oregon), Edwin G. Boring (Harvard University), James Bosma (National Institute of Dental Research), Michelangelo Fluckiger (with a recommendation from Jean Piaget), E. H. Gombrich, Mary Henle, Gunnar Johansson, David N. Lee, William Mace (University of Texas) Norman Malcolm (Cornell University), Frank J. Malina (founder and editor of LEONARDO magazine), Fabio Metelli (Italy), and Albert Michotte.
Joseph M. Notterman (Princeton Advisory Council), Joseph Royce (Center for Theoretical Psychology), and Robert E. Shaw (University of Connecticut). Also, Gibson books annotated by Gibson.
2 DVDs (DVD-914 and 915) containing videos of Remembering Gibson Symposium at International Conference on Perception and Action (ICPA), 2013.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Other Gibson material courtesy of Trinity College, including Remembering Gibson Symposium at International Conference on Perception and Action (ICPA) 2013 http://commons.trincoll.edu/isep

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: James J. Gibson papers, #14-23-1832. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a copy of the printed finding aid. Searchable PDF.

did.unittitle: Series I. Material prior to 1928 and Gibson's Professional Career

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material is arranged chronologically and include high school course books, course notes, and papers

did.unittitle: Card from New Trier Township High School

The Logical Foundation of Mathematics
Observations on the evolution of Language in the Light of Psychology

Wulf in Psychologische Forschung (translation)
Philogenesis of Labyrinthine Function
Perception of Form (History)
course book - History of Science
The Characteristics of Vertebrate Behavior
Physiology of the Labyrinth
General Notes, notes on the Epistemological Problems
Series II. Teaching and Professional material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material is arranged chronologically with folder titles and is divided into two subseries.

Psych. 511 Seminar on Perception
A Glossary of Terms used in Ecological Optics (second edition)
Memo on Motion
Two short papers - The Perception of Surface Layout. The Relation between Haptic and Visual Space
Two short notes on Ecological Optics
Three short papers - The Construction of Meaning vs. the Detection of Meaning. On the difference between Perception and Prioperception. The Puzzle of the Retinal Image.

Psych. 511 and 512 Perception - Psych. and Light (correspondence)
Psych. 512 Perception - Experiments on Direct Perception of Surface Layout (correspondence)
Psych. 512 Perception - Experiments on Direct Perception of Surface Layout (correspondence)
Two short notes - Information in Visual Theory. Outline of theory of direct visual Perception.

Three short notes - For a collection of papers in honor of Kai von Fieandt. A reconsideration of Eye-movements and eye postures based on Ecological Optics. A list of Ecologically valid meanings in a stationary ambient Optic Array.

Three short notes - Course of Readings for Grad. Students interested in advanced work in Perception. On the conflict between vision and Touch. References for Eye Movements and anomalies of Localization.
Two short notes - A further note on occlusion. The theory of Images transmitted to the brain.
Experiments on Perception Considered as Illusions
Two short papers - Homogeneous Optical Perception. Heterogeneous Optical Stimulation.
Two kinds of Equivocal Information in line drawings. Psych. 511 - The Image.
Two short papers - The Psychology of Representation. Does the ability to Visualize depend on Visual Images?

Three short papers - Reversible Perspective and Reversed Motion. The Perception of a Permanent WORLD (Psych. 511 - Memo) A suggested Classification of types and sub-types of graphic action (Memo for Psych. 511 "The Image")
Two short notes - Tentative Outline of History of the Concept of Images - Psych. 511. Transparency and Occlusion, or How Bishop Berkeley went wrong in the First Place.
Psych. 511 Perception THE IMAGE by A.G. Barrand (correspondence)
"Disparity" Paper (with annotations by Gibson, and a reprint)
"Disparity" Paper (with annotations by Gibson, and a reprint)
"Disparity" Paper (with annotations by Gibson, and a reprint)
General lectures on Visual Perception
Three short papers - A Reply to Johansson. The legacies of Koffka's Principles. The visual Ego (with reprints)
The relation between Hallucinations and Perception
Workshop on Ecological Optics (includes photos 4-93 Gunnar Jansson, Dolezal, Toleno, Hochberg. Also, large group second set: Harry Levin). See also: Series III, 1970 for paper on Ecological Optics
Workshop on Ecological Optics (includes photos 4-93 Gunnar Jansson, Dolezal, Toleno, Hochberg. Also, large group second set: Harry Levin). See also: Series III, 1970 for paper on Ecological Optics

A Terminology for describing the layout of Opaque surfaces and the Occluding of the surface by Another.
Three short papers - On the visual perception of Tangible and Intangible Things. The Information available in Pictorial displays. Note on Behavior and Koffka's Behavioral Environment (psych. 511)
Psych. 511 - Perception, Koffka
The relation between Retinal Stimulation and Visual Sensation.
Inquiry into Sensations - Seminar. The Problem of Event Perception.
Notes on radiant and ambient light and "natural Perception"
Psych. 512 - Ecological Optics

Four short papers - Anomalies of Form Perception. Loss of word-meaning with Prolonged Fixation. Concerning Onset and Cessation of Stimulation and Disturbances in an array of Stimulation. Current Problems in Ecological Optics - Psych. 512
A Preliminary Description and Classification of Affordances
Three short papers - Is there a Geometry for Ecological Optics? More on Affordances. Still more on Affordances.
Do we ever see light? The Problem of Information Pick-up - Psych. 512
On the distinction between Objects and Substances.
A Note on the Muddle of extrasensory Perception
The Crisis in Sensory Physiology
On the Concept of Optical Texture
Note on Terrestrial Orientation
A Note on Conjuring Tricks and the Psychology of Event Perception
Psych. 511 Seminar - Perception, Ecological Approach to Visual Perception (with correspondence)
"Form Perception without a Retinal Image" The Concept of Formless Invariants. (with reprint and correspondence)
Ryan on Perception of "Relevant" facts
Two short papers - Note on the differences between a sensory modality and a Perceptual System. Two Methods of describing Ambient Light.
Three short papers - Note on the Distinction between Stimulation and Stimulus Information. Note on the Concept of "Formless Invariants" in Visual Perception. Lecture on Occlusion in Perception
The Exploring, Selecting, and Enhancing of Optical Stimulus Information: A new classification of Ocular Adjustments.
A Note on the El Greco Fallacy
With what do we see?
Two short papers - On the concept of the "Visual solid Angle" in an optic Array and its History. On the Nature of Pictorial Representation.
Note on the Concept of "What is Given"
A Note on Visualizing Conceived as Visual Apprehending without any Particular Point of Observation.
Psych. 511 - Ecological Approach to Visual Perception
Psych. 512 - Affordances and the Problem of Meaning
What is meant by the Processing of Information?
A Classification of Environmental Events
Note on the Perception of Displacement
A further note on Formless invariants as optical information for Perception
Note on the conceptual muddle underlying the Optical Inversion Experiment
Psych. 511 The Processes of Visual Information Pick-up (with correspondence)
Overt and Covert Attention
A Neglected set of facts about vision that can only be comprehended by Ecological Optics
A Further Note on the Perceiving of Hidden and Unhidden Surfaces - Psych 512
Psych. 512 - Note on the Act of Orienting and the state of being Oriented (J. J. Gibson/David DeVilliers)
The Perceiving of the Hidden - A Tentative set of Theories
The theory of Proprioception and its Relation to Volition, an attempt at Clarification
Two short papers - The Puzzle of Optical Structure. A Note on current theories of Perception.
Note on Perceiving in a Populated Environment
A note on the relation between perceptual and conceptual knowledge
Two short papers - A listing of supposed operations on the data of sense. A note on the directness of Perception.
Note on the norms of surface layout
Psych. 512 The Processes of Information Pick-up
Photocopies of short papers and notations from Bill Adams, Howard Flock, and Julian Hochberg with annotations by Gibson.

Two short papers - Affordances and Behavior. The psychophysical Experiment and the Perception Exp.
What is the relation of concepts to percepts
Note for a tentative redefinition of behavior
Note on proprioperception in relation to somaesthesis, self-awareness, and introspection.
On the new idea of persistence and change and the old ideas that give it drives out.
Psych. 511 - Perception - Cognition and attention, in the perception of the environment (comments on drafts of Ulric Neisser, Cognition and Reality)
Psych. 511 - Perception - Cognition and attention, in the perception of the environment (comments on drafts of Ulric Neisser, Cognition and Reality)
Philosophy 437c and Psych. 401b - Ecological Approach to Perception
Note on the apprehension of Formless and Timeless Invariants
Two short papers - Note on some types of visualizing considered as extended Forms of Visual Perceiving. A note on the Argument from Equivalent Configuration
What is it to Perceive?

Two papers - Notes on Theory and Experiments in Pictorially Mediated Perception or Perplexities and Fallacies in the study of Pictures. (with correspondence) Memo on the process of perception invariance Detection.
Two papers - Theory of Affordances and Design of the Environment. Five kinds of knowing.
Symposium (Society for Philos. and Psych.) Ted Mischel (chair), Hamlyn vs. Gibson.
Psych. 510- Perception - The problems of Pictorial Mediation in Perception (includes reprint)
Psych. 511 - Perception - On the relation between seeing, perceiving, and knowing (with correspondence)
Psych. 511 - Perception - (includes reprint and correspondence)
Anthony Barrand, An Ecological Approach to Binocular Perception, The Neglected Facts of Occlusion (includes reprint, correspondence)
Preliminary Tabulation of Processes that have been supposed to Mediate Perception.
lecture at Boston University - Perception and Pictures (reprints and correspondence)
A note on direct Perception. Various kinds of Indirect Apprehension, and Degrees of Indirectness
On the analysis of Change in the Optic Array (published)
A Tentative Formula for the Perception of Persistence and the awareness of Reality
Psych. 512 - Perception - The Theory of Processing and the theory of Pick-up
Psych. 231 - University of California - (Davis), Perception (includes correspondence)
Talks given by Gibson in California, Stanford University, Santa Cruz, Davis, University of California at Berkeley
Psych. 511 - Perception and Orientation (reprint, correspondence)
The Dependence of Perception on Locomotion
Memo on Vision and Touch considered as Perceptual Systems. A note on what exists at the Ecological level of Reality
Seminar at Salk, at Cornell (with reprint)
Ambulatory Vision and Knowledge of One's Habitat (at University of California at San Diego)
Notes for Seminar at UCSD, A Terminology for Discussing Images (the Image-Concept)
lecture for Pat Carlson, Kurt Koffka and the Roots of Ecological Psych. (includes correspondence)
What is involved in surface Perception? (draft for Padua Conference)
A note on substances, surfaces, places, objects, events
Two short papers - Various kinds of Cognition. The Gap in the Ecological Approach.
Seminar on Magic (includes correspondence)
Ecological Physics, Magic and Reality
Two short papers - Conclusion from a century of Research on Sense Perception (APA). The Perception of Surfaces vs. The Perception of markings on a Surface.
Psych. 511 - Perception - Visual Perception, Visual Kinesthesis, and the control of action.
Psych. 511 - Perception - Visual Perception, Visual Kinesthesis, and the control of action.
Prelim - Some distinctions for cognitive terms on decorative art
Jean Graham's report on optical Tunnel and Photo Illustrations (with visual material)
Notes for the Affordance theory of Meaning

CIO PAC propaganda
CIO PAC propaganda
CIO PAC propaganda
Principles of propaganda technique
Series III. Material for Publication

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material is arranged chronologically and includes articles, draft copies of book material, essays, short papers, visual material (such as diagrams, original sketches, photographs, and tables).

did.unittitle: Notes for General articles on dimensions and norms in Perception (with reprints)
Motion picture testing and research, Aviation Psych. Research Reports, no. 7 (photos)
Motion picture testing and research, Aviation Psych. Research Reports, no. 7 (photos)
Motion picture testing and research, Aviation Psych. Research Reports, no. 7 (photos)
PTFU Research reports on Aircraft Recognition Training
Miscellaneous terms
"Size Constancy and Visual Acuity"
Research Bulletin - Test film unit
Aircraft Recognition
diagrams - Fig. 1. to accompany Gibson and Gagne's Learning to Recognize Aircraft -- The Nature of remembered shapes etc. Page 7
diagrams - Fig. 7-A and 7B, 7.2, 7.3, (from same title as above)
Sketch of a theory of Learning for Soc. Psych.
The Perception of Visual Surfaces (with correspondence)
clippings, The Perception of the Visual World
illustrations for The Perception of the Visual World
photos for The Perception of the Visual World
sketches for The Perception of the Visual World
The Identification Experiment Notes for Paper and APA conference (with correspondence)
Picture Perception - correspondence, drafts, notes, and revisions
Picture Perception - correspondence, drafts, notes, and revisions
"The Visual Perception of Motion in Space a Survey of the Problems"
Notes and Translations on Space Perception, Gibson and Purdy
"Social Perception and the Psychology of Perceptual Learning"
"Motion Parallax and Motion perceptive during Aircraft Landings" Gibson, Paul Olum, Frank Rosenblatt
"The Result of Homogeneous Retinal Stimulation for Visual Perception" with Dickins Waddell
A Tentative set of Postulates for a theory of Picture-Perception (prepublication draft) A Theory of Pictorial Perception (published title)
Notes on Ecological Optics (includes reprints)
"Accuracy in the Visual Perception of Speed," Smith, Smith, and Gibson
The Optical Expansion-Pattern in Aerial Locomotion
Studies on Aircraft Landing - Response to E. S. Calvert (Royal Aircraft Establishment, Farnborough) (with correspondence)
"Visually controlled Locomotion and Visual Orientation in Animals" (with correspondence"
Perception (from the Encyclopedia of Sci. and Technology)
"The Info. Required for the Visual Control of Aircraft Landings" and Research on the Visual Perception of Motion and Change"
Report to Gordon: Helicopter Simulator Report Gibson and Smith
chapters 2-7 from The World and The Senses (preliminary title for The Senses Considered as Perceptual Systems) (chapter 7 includes figures)
chapters 2-7 from The World and The Senses (preliminary title for The Senses Considered as Perceptual Systems) (chapter 7 includes figures)
chapters 2-7 from The World and The Senses (preliminary title for The Senses Considered as Perceptual Systems) (chapter 7 includes figures)
chapters 2-7 from The World and The Senses (preliminary title for The Senses Considered as Perceptual Systems) (chapter 7 includes figures)
chapters 2-7 from The World and The Senses (preliminary title for The Senses Considered as Perceptual Systems) (chapter 7 includes figures)
short papers entitled: Exploration by the Ocular System

"Observations of Active Touch"
"The Concept of the Stimulus in Psych."
The Contribution of Experimental Psych. to the formulation of the problem of safety
An After effect in Haptic Space Perception

Notes on the Auditory System Ch. V (with correspondence)
Notes on the Haptic System Ch. VI
Notes for an Ecological Approach to Visual Perception
Gibson and Stanley: Form Perception with three types of Tactual Input (with correspondence)
Further Implications of "Explorations in Active Touch"
short papers
Manuscript on Depth Perception (for Encyclopedia Social Science)
A Classification of Illusions - Notes on deficiency of perception (with reprint)
Notes on Ecological Optics (basis for "new" book on vision) (with reprints)
Gibson and West - The Artificial "Looker" (with reprint)
Gibson and West - The Artificial "Looker" (with reprint)
Notes on Occlusion and Disocclusion (correspondence and reprints)
Notes on Occlusion and Disocclusion (correspondence and reprints)
Notes for a possible chapter on sensation
Manuscript for Journal of Experimental Child Psych. (correspondence and reprints)
Summary of the Investigations made under Research Career Award
Notes on Color Vision (with correspondence and reprints)
The relation of visual sensations to visual perception
Constancy and Invariance in Perception, with notes and correspondence
Memoranda on Ecological Optics
Memoranda on Ecological Optics

Photocopies of illustrations from The Senses
Notes for introduction to The Senses

The Stick-in-Water Problem (with correspondence and reprints)
The Analysis of Reading Skill Progress Report (with correspondence)
"Scribbling Paper" (with correspondence)
Glossary of Terms used in Ecological Optics from The Senses
"New Reasons for Realism"

Miscellaneous notes on Perception
Notes for the Evidence for the Direct Perception of the layout of surfaces (ch. Part III a) (with reprint)
Notes on a Visual Control of Manipulation (with Pat Yonas)
Following In Chicks, Peek-a-boo in babies
An outline of experiments on the Direct Perception of Surface Layout
The Legacies of Koffka's Principles
"A Theory of Direct Visual Perception"
Notes on the stimulus (with reprints)

Series IV. Reprints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The reprinted material is arranged in two subseries.

did.unittitle: Reprints by James J. Gibson are arranged chronologically with a listing of their titles in this index.
Gibson, J. J. The reproduction of visually perceived forms. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1929, 12, 1-39
Gibson, J. J. , Jack, E. G., & Raffel, G. Bilateral transfer of the conditioned response in the human subject. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1932, 15, 416-421
Gibson, J. J. Adaption, after-effect and contrast in the perception of curved lines. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1933, 16, 1-31
Gibson, J. J. Retroaction and the method of recognition. Journal of General Psychology. 1934, 10, 234-236
Gibson, J. J., & Gibson, E. J. Retention and the interpolated task. American Journal of Psychology, 1934, 46, 603-610
Gibson, J. J., & Hudson, L. Bilateral transfer of the conditioned knee-jerk. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1935, 18, 774-783.
Gibson, J. J., & Robinson, D. Orientation in visual perception: The recognition of familiar plane forms in differing orientations. Psychological Monographs, 1935, 46, (6 Whole No. 210, 39-47).
Gibson, (Ed.) Studies in psychology from Smith College. Psychological Monographs, 1935, 6, No. 210, pp. 98.
Gibson, J. J. A note on the conditioning of voluntary reactions. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1936, 19, 397-399
Gibson, J. J. Review of E. Freeman, Social Psychology. Psychological Bulletin, 1936, 33, 664-666
Gibson, J. J., & Raffel, G. A technique for investigating retroactive and other inhibitory effects in immediate memory. Journal of General Psychology, 1936, 15, 107-116.

Gibson, J. J. Adaption, after-effect and contrast in the perception of tilted lines: II. Simultaneous contrast and the areal restriction of the after-effect. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1937, 20, 553-569
Gibson, J. J. Adaption with negative after-effect. Psychological Review, 1937, 44, 222-244
Gibson, J. J., & Radner, M. Adaption, after-effect and contrast in the perception of tilted lines: I. Quantitative studies. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1937, 20, 453-467
Gibson, J. J., & Crooks, L. E. A theoretical field analysis of automobile-driving. American Journal of Psychology, 1938, 51, 453-471.
Gibson, J. J., & Mowrer, O. H. Determinants of the perceived vertical and horizontal. Psychological Review, 1938, 45, 300-323
Gibson, J. J. Smith College Driving Test
Gibson, J. J. The Aryan myth. Journal of Educational Sociology, 1939, 13, 164-171
Gibson, J. J. Why a union for teachers? Focus, 1939, 2, 3-7
Gibson, J. J. A critical review of the concept of set in contemporary experimental psychology. Psychological Bulletin, 1941, 38, 781-817
Gibson, J. J. "Application of the concept of Perceptual Constancy to Aviation," Psychology section (FTC)
Gibson, J. J. The Aviation Psychology Program of the Army Air Forces, Psychological Bulletin Vol. 40, No. 10, Dec. 1943
Gibson, J. J. Organization and Research Activities Psychological Research Unit No. 3, Army Air Forces, Psychological Bulletin, Vol. 41, No. 4, April 1944
Gibson, J. J. Psychological Activities in the Training Command, Air Forces, Psychological Bulletin, Vol. 42, No. 1, January 1945
Gibson, J. J. History, organization, and research activities of the Psychological Test Film Unit, Army Air Forces. Psychological Bulletin, 1944, 457-568.
Gibson, J. J. Studying perceptual phenomena. In T. G. Andrews (Ed.), Methods of Psychology, N.Y.: Wiley, 1948
Gibson, J. J. The perception of visual surfaces. American Journal of Psychology, 1950, 63, 367-384
What is a form? Psychological Review, 1951, 58, 403-412
Gibson, J. J. Theories of perception. In W. Dennis (Ed.), Current trends in Psychological theory. Pittsburgh: University of Pittsburgh Press, 1951
Gibson, J. J. The visual field and the visual world: A reply to Professor Boring. Psychological Review, 1952, 59, 149-151.
Gibson, J. J. The relation between visual and postural determinants of the phenomenal vertical. Psychological Review, 1952, 59, 370-375
Gibson, J. J. & Carel, W. Does motion perspective independently produce the impression of a receding surface? Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1952, 44, 16-18
Gibson, J. J., & Cornsweet, J. The perceived slant of visual surfaces - optical and geographical. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1952, 44, 11-15.
Gibson, J. J. & Dibble, F. N. Exploratory experiments on the stimulus conditions for the perception of a visual surface. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1952, 43, 414-419
Gibson, J. J., & Waddell, D. Homogeneous retinal stimulation and visual perception. American Journal of Psychology, 1952, 65, 263-370.
Gibson, J. J. Ordinal stimulation and the possibility of a global psychophysics. Proceedings of the 14th International Congress of Psychology. Amsterdam: North-Holland, 1954
Gibson, J. J. The visual perception of objective motion and subjective movement. Psychological Review, 1954, 61, 304-314
Gibson, J. J. A theory of pictorial perception. Audio-Visual Communication Review, 1954, 1, 3-23
Gibson, J. J. Review of M. D. Vernon, A further study of visual perception. Psychological Bulletin, 1954, 51, 96-97
Gibson, J. J. The optical expansion pattern in aerial locomotion. American Journal of Psychology, 1955, 68, 480-484
Beck, J., & Gibson, J. J. The relation of apparent shape to apparent slant in the perception of objects. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1955, 50, 125-133.
Gibson, J. J., & Gibson, E. J. Perceptual learning: Differentiation or enrichment? Psychological Review, 1955, 62, 32-41.
Gibson, J. J., & Gibson, E. J. What is learned in Perceptual learning? A Reply to Professor Postman. Psychological Review, 1955, 62, 447-450.
Gibson, J. J., Olum, P.. & Rosenblatt, F. Parallax and perceptive during aircraft landings. American Journal of Psychology, 1955, 68, 372-385.
Gibson, J. J., Purdy, J., & Lawrence, L. A method of controlling stimulation for the study of space perception: The optical tunnel. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1955, 50, 1-14.
Gibson, J. J. The non-projective aspects of the Rorschach experiment: IV. The Rorschach blots considered as pictures. Journal of Social Psychology, 1956, 44, 203-206.
Gibson, J. J., & Smith, O. W. Apparatus for the study of visual translator motion. American Journal of Psychology, 1957, 70, 291-294
Gibson, J. J., & Gibson, E. J. Continuous perception transformations and the perception of rigid motion. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1957, 54, 129-138.
Gibson, J. J., Smith, O. W., Steinschneider, A., & Johnson, C. W. The relative accuracy of visual perception of motion during fixation and pursuit. American Journal of Psychology, 1957, 70, 64-68
Gibson, J. J. Optical motions and transformations as stimuli for visual perception. Psychological Review, 1957, 64, 288-295.
Gibson, J. J. Technical and scientific communication: A reply to Calvert. American Journal of Psychology, 1957, 70, 129-131.

Gibson, J. J. The registering of objective facts: An interpretation of Woodworth's theory of perceiving. In G. Seward & J. Seward (Eds.) Current psychological issues: Essays in honor of Robert S. Woodworth. New York: Holt, 1958
Gibson, J. J. Visually controlled locomotion and visual orientation in animals. British Journal of Psychology, 1958, 49, 182-194.

Gibson, J. J. Research on the visual perception of motion and change. In Second Symposium on Physiological Psychology (ONR Symposium Report ACR-30). Washington, D. C.: Office of Naval Research, 1958. Reprinted in I. Spigel (Ed.), Readings in the study of visually perceived movement. New York: Harper & Row, 1965.
Gibson, J. J. After-effects: Figural and negative. Review of P. McEwen, Figural after-effects. Contemporary Psychology, 1959, 3, 294-295.
von Fieandt, K., & Gibson, J. J. The sensitivity of the eye to two kinds of continuous transformation of a shadow-pattern. Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1959, 57, 344-347.
Bergman, R., & Gibson, J. J. The negative after-effect of the perception of a surface slanted in the third dimension. American Journal of Psychology, 1959, 72, 364-374.
Gibson, E. J. & Gibson, J. J., Smith, O. W., & Flock, H. Motion parallax as a determinant of perceived depth. Journal of experiential Psychology, 1959, 58, 40-51
Gibson, J. J. Perception as a function of stimulation. In S. Koch (Ed.), Psychology: A study of a science. New York: McGraw-Hill, 1959.
Gibson, J. J. Pictures, perspective, and perception. Daedalus, 1960, 216-227.
Gibson, J. J. The information contained in light. Acta Psychologica, 1960, 17, 23-30.
Gibson, J. J. The concept of the stimulus in psychology. American Psychologist, 1960, 16, 694-703.
Gibson, J. J. Distinguished Scientific Contribution Awards, American Psychologist, Vol. 16, No. 12, 1961.

Gibson, J. J. The contribution of experimental psychology to the formulation of the problem of safety: A brief for basic research. In Behavioral approaches to accident research. New York: Association for the Aid of Crippled Children, 1961
Gibson, J. J. Ecological Optics. Vision Research, 1961, 1, 253-262.
Gibson, E. J., Gibson, J. J., Pick, A.D., Osser, H. A developmental study of the discrimination of letter-like forms. Journal of Comparative and Psychological Psychology, 1962, 55, 897-906.
Gibson, J. J. Observations on active touch. Psychological Review, 1962, 69, 477-491.
Gibson, J. J. The survival value of sensory systems. Biological prototypes and synthetic systems (Vol. 1.) New York: Plenum, 1962
Gibson, J. J., & Flock, H. The apparent distance of mountains. American Journal of Psychology, 1962, 75, 501-503.
Gibson, J. J., Schiff, W., & Caviness, J. Persistent fear responses in rhesus monkeys to the optical stimulus of "looming." Science, 1962, 136, 982-983.
Gibson, J. J. The useful dimensions of sensitivity. American Psychologist, 1963, 18, 1-15.
Gibson, J. J., & Backlund, F. An after-effect in haptic space perception. Quarterly Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1963, 15, 145-154.
Gibson, J. J., & Pick, A. D. Perception of another person's looking behavior. American Journal of Psychology, 1963, 76, 386-394.
Gibson, J. J. Research on the Visual Perception of Motion and Change, from Readings in the Study of Visually Perceived Movement, Harper and Row, New York, 1965
Gibson, J. J. James J. Gibson. In E.G. Boring and G. Lindzey (Eds.), A history of psychology in autobiography (Vol.5) New York: Appleton-Century-Crofts, 1967
Gibson, J. J. On the proper meaning of the term "stimulus." Psychological Review, 1967, 74, 533-534

Gibson, J. J. Invariant properties of changing stimulation as information for perception. In F. Klix (Ed.), The organization of human information processing: Symposium von XVII Internationalen Kongress fur Psychologie. Berlin: Akademie-Verlag, 1967.
Gibson, J. J. New reasons for realism. Synthese, 1967, 17, 162-172
Gibson, J. J. What gives rise to the perception of motion? Psychological Review, 1968, 75, 335-346.
Gibson, J. J. Depth perception. In International Encyclopedia of the Social Sciences. New York: Macmillan, 1968
Gibson, J. J. The change from visible to invisible: A study of optical transitions Perception and Psychophysics. with G. Kaplan, H. Reynolds, and K. Wheeler, pp. 113-116.
Reprints from SCIENCE, review of Baird, John, C., "Psychophysical Analysis of Visual Space."
Gibson, J. J. Are there sensory qualities of objects? Synthese, 1968-1969, 19, 408-409.

Gibson, J. J. Further thoughts on the perception of rigid motion. In J. Jarvinen (Ed.), Contemporary research in psychology of perception: In honorem Kai von Fiendt Sexagenarii. Porvoo, Finland: Werner Soderstrom Osakeyhtio, 1969
Gibson, J. J. On theories for visual space perception: A reply to Johansson. Scandinavian Journal of Psychology, 1970, 11, 75-79.
Gibson, J. J. On the relation between hallucination and perception. Leonardo, 1970, 3, 425-427.
Gibson, J. J. The information available in Pictures. Leonardo, 1971, 4, 27-35
Gibson, J. J. The legacies of Koffka's Principles. Journal for the History of the Behavioral Sciences, 1971, 7, 3-9.
Gibson, J. J. A theory of direct visual perception. In J. Royce & W. Rozeboom (Eds.), Psychology of knowing. New York: Gordon & Breach, 1972.
Gibson, J. J. On the concept of "formless invariants" in visual perception. Leonardo, 1973, 6, 43-45.
Gibson, J. J. Direct visual perception: A reply to Gyr. Psychological Bulletin, 1973, 79, 396-397.
Gibson, J. J. A note on ecological optics. In E. Carterette & M. Friedman (Eds.) Handbook of Perception (Vol. 1). New York: Academic Press, 1974.
Gibson, J. J. Visualizing conceived as visual apprehending without any particular point of observation. Leonardo, 1974, 7, 41-42.
Gibson, J. J. "Comment to Rochi, Vasco," Leonardo, letters col. Vol. 7, 1974.
Gibson, J. J. Events are perceivable but time is not. In J. T. Fraser & N. Lawrence (Eds.), The study of time, II. New York: Springer-Verlag, 1975.
Gibson, J. J. Pickford and the failure of experimental esthetics. Leonardo, 1975, 8, 319-321.

Gibson, J. J. The implications of experience on the perception of space and motion. Office of Naval Research final Report (Contract No. N000 14-67A-0077-0005). Arlington, Va.: Office of Naval Research (Environmental Physiology), 1975.

Gibson, J. J. Three kinds of distance that can be seen, or how Bishop Berkeley went wrong. In G. B. Flores D'Arcais (Ed.), Studies in Perception: Festschrift for Fabio Metelli. Milano-Firenze: Giunte Editore, 1976.
Gibson, J. J. The myth of passible perception: A reply to Richards. Philosophy and Phenomenological Research, 1976, 37, 234-238.
Gibson, J. J. "Comments on Maurin's Discussion of Visual Perception Hypotheses," Leonardo, letters col. Vol. 9, 1976.
Gibson, J. J. On the analysis of change in the optic array. Scandinavian Journal of Psychology, 1977, 18, 161-163.
Gibson, J. J. The theory of affordances. In R. Shaw & J. Bransford (Eds.), Perceiving, acting and knowing: Towards an ecological psychology. Hillsdale, N. J.: Erlbaum, 1977.
Gibson J. J. The ecological approach to the visual perception of pictures. Leonardo, 1978, 11, 227-235.
Gibson, J. J. An interview: In D. Ecker and S. Madeja, Pioneers in Perception: A Study of Aesthetic Perception, St. Louis: Cermel, Inc., 1979, 89-142.
Reprints by other authors are arranged chronologically with a listing of their full names in this box
Samuel Fernberger (1941), Olin Smith (1956), Julian Hochberg (1957), Walter Cohen (1957, 1958)
Fred Ficucia (1960), E. H. Gombrich (1960), Contemporary Psychology (vol. 5, no. 8, Aug. 1960), Rudolf Arnheim (review, 1962), Julian Hochberg/ Virginia Brooks (1962)
Gunnar Johansson "Event Perception"
Howard R. Flock (1963, 1964), E. H. Gombrich (1964), Samuel C. McLaughlin (1964), David H. Hubel/ Torsten N. Wiesel (1964)
Jacob Beck, William Schiff, Robert B. Freeman, Jr., Anne D. Pick, Howard R. Flock
Roy Mefferd, Jr. (et. al.), T. G. Sadler (et. al.), Carl E. Sherrick, John C. Hay, Madjid Mashhour, Robert B. Freeman, Jr., Julian Hochberg, Olin W. Smith/Patricia C. Smith
D. E. Broadbent, William Schiff/Herbert Isikow
William N. Hayes/Edward I. Saiff, E. Sture Eriksson, Norman Malcolm, S. S. Greenberg
Jan B. Deregowski, E. Sture Eriksson, John C. Hay, N. S. Sutherland
Jake Beck
Charles S. Singleton (1969), Ernst Poppel (1969), Ernst Poppel/Henner Giedke (1970)
R. K. Davenport/C. M. Rogers (1971), E. M. Gombrich (1972)
Jacqueline Metzler/Roger N. Shepard, Lynn A. Cooper/Roger N. Shepard
University of Uppsala: Sverker Runeson, Gunnar Johansson, Kare Rumar, Ulf Berggrund
Science (Jan. 26, 1973-vol. 179, no. 4071, James T. Walker, Albert S. Bergman/Andre Achim
Vasco Ronchi (1974), Lars Gunnar Lundh (1974), David N. Lee (1975), Graphics Newsletter (1975/1976), Rudolf Modley (1976), American Psychologist (May 1979)
Series V. Material of an experimental nature, draft notations for later work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material is arranged chronologically with folder titles and includes research files, notes, and draft copies (mostly unpublished material)

did.unittitle: Gestalt Theory of Perception
The Reaction Experiment
Experimental Problems - Perceptual Adaptation (with paper by Fritz Heider)
Experimental Problems - Learning and Memory
Notes, projects on simultaneous contrast for curvature, The Contrast Phenomenon
Experimental Problems - attitude, meaning, motivation, problems, reasoning, thought
Notes on books, Personality and Tests
Notes on Constancy - Visual forms as variables
Field-Driving
Projects and Notes on Tilt - adaption and negative after-effect
Notes on psychophysical theory of perception (notes from ch. 1 and ch. 5)
Notes on Freudian theory, abnormal theory
Notes on Postural and Visual Orientation and Space Perception (notes from article with Mowre) The Spatial Ego
Notes on voluntary set (in relation to conditioning - voluntary-involuntary dichotomy) includes correspondence
Notes on the nature tasks and task-sets
Stereotyped Gestures (hands and arms) data, film, notes includes correspondence
Stereotyped Gestures (hands and arms) data, film, notes includes correspondence
Notes on set as a determinant of perception (and memory and thought)
Notes on Learning and Intention. Association and neutral set
Kinesthetic Horizontal Experiment. Wardell on Kinesthetic and Visual Adaption
Notes on set
Test sheets
Conceptualization and reproduction of non-sense forms, Wheeler
Notes on form-perception. Past experience versus structural determinants (with correspondence)
Distance Estimation Research (Distance Estimation Test C P212A)
Notes on "Landing Cues" (with correspondence)
Notes on Texture and Acuity

Estimation of Velocity Test CP 205B-I (answer sheets), Identification of Velocities Test CP 205B-II, Flexibility of Attention Test CP 411 E, Plane Formation Test CP 805C, Integration of Attention Test CP 415A
Meaning of correlation coefficients (and stanines)
intercorrelations and factor analyses of Perceptual and Motion Picture tests.
Motion Picture Tests, keys
Notes for applications to art Psychological problems in Visual Art, also Socio-psychological problems in art.
Minimal Movement Test CP213C, Drift Direction Test CP221B, Estimation of Relative Velocities Test CP205B III, Tests. Answer sheets
Minimal Movement B1 Statistics and Validation Data
Landing Judgement Test CP505E
Film Industry Motion Picture Test (with correspondence)
Drawing Experiment - Remembered shapes of Aircraft
Successive Perception Test I, and II CP509C answer sheets
Flying Orientation Test CP 107A
Notes on Physiological Optics (including Eye Movements and the stable world)
Notes on the theory of perception (and the theories)
Potential Experiments on Space Perception
Notes on Meaning in relation to Perception (Mary Henle's experiment)
Notes on Learning in relation to Perception ("Innate perceptions) notes for ch. 12
Potential Experiments on gradients and Surface Perception
Notes, Experiments on Orientation (with correspondence and reprints)
Apparatus Experiment and materials (with reprints and samples)
Draft reports, illustrations, and notes on optical tunnel (with correspondence
Draft reports, illustrations, and notes on optical tunnel (with correspondence
Stimuli, for information for margin on contour (with correspondence)
Optical Tunnel Experiments - reports on J.Beck (with correspondence)
Reports and Notes on Perceptive transformations and "kinetic" depth (with reprints)
Notes on Perception, Perceptual Learning and Memory (Koch's volumes 4, 5, 6 with reprints)
An attempt to measure the distinctiveness of wines
Notes on Pictures and Perspective (Panofsky's paper) (includes correspondence and reprints)
Space and Motion Experiments to be tried (ONR contract)
Notes on Optical Motions (with reprints)
Digestion and Indigestion in the Assimilation of culture
Outline of a new attempt to classify the senses and the sensory inputs
Tactual Perception (ONR research) (with correspondence)
Notes for the useful Dimensions of Sensitivity (APA address and the Ms.)
Notes on "Sensations and 'What are they'?" (with reprint)
Unpublished draft: Four cases of the perception of size in space
Oral fee lies (includes example sets of feelies and correspondence)
Experimental reports: Exploratory vs. Performatory Acts
Notes on the chemical value system
Oral feelies
Visual motions and transformations (notes) The temporal order problem. Includes comments on Appendix of David Bohm
Visual motions and transformations (notes) The temporal order problem.
General notes for book in 1966
Notes on stimulus ecology and meaningful stimulation (including evolution of cognition)
Draft copy of "Optical Occlusion and Edge - Information in an Optic Array"
How do we see to do things? Visual Proprioception with monograph
Notes for Review of Savage (with reprint)
Notes on Hallucination (with reprint)
Notes on the perceptual-behavioral phenomena of orientation (with correspondence and reprints)
Notes on the perceptual-behavioral phenomena of orientation (with correspondence and reprints)
Notes on the perceptual-behavioral phenomena of orientation (with correspondence and reprints)
Phenomenal Time vs. Physical time (space-time and Relativity - as based on the speed of light)
Jerry Cline on Distance Perception on Pictures which are impoverished
Perception of Visual Surfaces II
Notes and suggestions on curvature experiment (including curvature adaption with line patterns) (with visual material)
Depth-Shape: The problem of Protuberance and Indentation
Notes on Merleau Ponty
Location of an object on a background (includes photos)
Morality for children - Elements of naturalist ethics
Photos of superposition, perspective scenes, and Optical Tunnel
Unpublished paper: Sensory Cues and Dynamic Distortion in a Helicopter Flight Simulator
Series VI. Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence is arranged by subject with inclusive dates in twelve subseries
